nodes.label: AI as lesson planning assistant for initial ideas
nodes.label_negative: AI as source of inappropriate or unrealistic suggestions
nodes.grounding_span: AI will usually give me lesson ideas that are not appropriate for my classroom specifically

nodes.label: AI as content fact-checker
nodes.label_negative: AI as provider of inaccurate information
nodes.grounding_span: fact check content information when I am unsure

nodes.label: AI as practice problem generator
nodes.grounding_span: I will just use it to generate practice problems since that is what it is best at

nodes.label: AI as diagram creator for simple images
nodes.label_negative: AI as incapable of complex accurate diagrams
nodes.grounding_span: AI can often create those quickly for me. However, it has been incapable of making accurate images that require any complexity

nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool for time-consuming tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to human connection and authority in teaching
nodes.grounding_span: It may help speed up some of the more time-consuming tasks, but it cannot engage with students in the same way as a person

nodes.label: AI use for grading and student feedback
nodes.label_negative: Human-only grading to respect privacy and provide genuine feedback
nodes.grounding_span: I do not use AI for grading, as I feel that it would infringe on privacy as well as an obligation for me to give students the respect of looking at their work and providing genuine feedback

nodes.label: AI-generated material transparency for students
nodes.grounding_span: students need to understand the difference between AI generated material and human creations

nodes.label: Effective lesson planning and content accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: fact check content information when I am unsure

nodes.label: Student privacy and respect
nodes.grounding_span: infringe on privacy as well as an obligation for me to give students the respect of looking at their work and providing genuine feedback

nodes.label: Student critical thinking and discernment
nodes.grounding_span: students need to understand the difference between AI generated material and human creations, and they should be able to critically examine work accordingly

nodes.label: Human connection and relationship in teaching
nodes.grounding_span: Human connection and relationships are needed in order to genuinely reach these students and encourage them to learn

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for simple diagram creation
nodes.grounding_span: AI can often create those quickly for me

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for complex diagrams
nodes.grounding_span: it has been incapable of making accurate images that require any complexity and/or labeling, and it can be frustrating

nodes.label: Reluctance to use AI for grading due to privacy and respect concerns
nodes.grounding_span: I do not use AI for grading, as I feel that it would infringe on privacy

nodes.label: Preference for iterative chunked interaction over single prompts
nodes.grounding_span: I find it to be more efficient to work in little chunks

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI replacing teachers due to need for human connection
nodes.grounding_span: I do not believe that AI could reasonably take over the duties of a public school teacher

nodes.label: Iterative refinement and chunking — breaks tasks into small back-and-forth steps
nodes.grounding_span: I find it to be more efficient to work in little chunks

nodes.label: Contextual adaptation — tailors AI suggestions to specific classroom realities
nodes.grounding_span: I have to tweak them significantly before implementation
